programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a pharmacist is found shot to death in his Wisconsin farmhouse, investigators embark on a 15 year hunt for his elusive killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is killed while walking her dogs in the woods. Since she had no enemies and her husband has an alibi, police assume it was a hunting accident...until a strange letter turns up allegedly written by the woman before her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Computers aid the police and FBI in solving a string of crimes in St. Louis.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teen was killed by her parents in what they said was self-defense, but forensic evidence told otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Scientists and detectives use DNA to solve a string of rapes and murders.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murders of an elderly couple leave police stumped until, two years later, they receive a major break. A jeweler looking through old newspaper clippings recognizes a necklace the female victim wore as having been pawned in her own shop.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Sheila Bryan, who was convicted of killing her mother in a car fire, is granted a new trial. An expert fire witness's opinion on how the fire ignited differs from the prosecution's theory. If verified, it would clear Bryan of any culpability.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: With no forensic evidence inside a murder scene, investigators were baffled. But they suspected that the victim's dog had witnessed the crime. If she had, forensic scientists would have to find a way to find out what the dog had seen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The flu-like symptoms of a mother and her children proved to be indicative of something much more serious: thallium poisoning. Investigators had to find the source of the poison. When the mother died, the needed to determine if the exposure was accidental or if they also needed to find a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A routine missing persons report leads Texas police to discover a deadly love triangle; evidence found in a suspect's home matches the murder scene and leads to the indictment of the victim's girlfriend and her lover.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Time of death becomes pivotal after a pregnant woman is found murdered in her air-conditioned bedroom. A striking similarity between her death and an HBO movie gives examiners the clues they need to thaw out the alibi of a cold-blooded killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For six years, a serial killer prowled the streets of New York City. He wrote letters to police and The New York Post, indicating he would kill twelve people, one for each astrological sign.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In this classic episode of Forensic Files, the longest running true crime series in television history, Eileen and Derrick Severs disappeared from their home in the small village of Hambleton in Great Britain, and police found evidence which suggested foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While Earl Morris was vacationing in California, he learned his wife had gone missing from their home in Arizona. The search for Ruby Morris involved dozens of investigators and scientists, even the coast guard. And the results of that investigation surprised everyone... especially Earl Morris.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 34-year-old nurse experiences a variety of flu-like symptoms. None of her doctors are able to discover the cause, until she visits the gynecologist for a routine check-up. Then she learns it's something far worse than the flu.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Details the disappearance of Melissa Brannen, a five-year-old girl who went missing from a Christmas party in 1989. The fibers on her clothes were the only means for police to determine her abductor. Police grew suspicious of Cal Hughes who was washing his clothes at 1am when they came to his home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A routine missing persons report leads Texas police to discover a deadly love triangle; evidence found in a suspect's home matches the murder scene and leads to the indictment of the victim's girlfriend and her lover.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The death of a man suffering from depression is initially considered a suicide, but further police investigation points to his wife.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are dispatched to the scene of a shooting, and discover the victim's husband is one of their own: a homicide detective who says his wife accidentally shot herself in the head.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The police investigate when a severed leg is discovered in the garbage. Conventional means of identifying the victim are impossible, leading investigators to rely on DNA, forensic anthropology and toxicology to identify the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man disappears one year to the day after beginning an affair with his friend's wife. 15 years later, with the help of a metal detector, police found evidence which would prove what had happened, and who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a human skull retrieved from an Ohio pond reveals a ghastly crime. Markings on the skull indicate that the victim had been stabbed multiple times and that the teeth had been removed with needle-nose pliers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the apparent overdose suicide of Reverend Bill Guthrie's wife Sharon. Detectives suspect foul play after they recover incriminating files from Bill's computer and learn of his infidelity.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspect's former lover comes forward with a fantastic tale of murder, mutilation and cremation, but there's no way to test the story's validity until a plant pathologist and a dendrochronology's conduct tests where the cremation supposedly occurred

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a day of fishing in a small, quiet village in Switzerland, a teenage boy did not return home as planned. The investigation revealed some important microscopic evidence in the water near where he was last seen. It was the only forensic evidence detectives had. But would it be enough for them to find him?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation of the kidnapping of a prominent banker's wife led police on a wild goose chase. An unexpected discovery made by an entomologist led to a new suspect, and the realization that ordinary office equipment can leave extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is killed while walking her dogs in the woods. Since she had no enemies and her husband has an alibi, police assume it was a hunting accident...until a strange letter turns up allegedly written by the woman before her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For seven years, a trio of men robbed one bank after another. They always got away before the police arrived, and they left no evidence behind. But the way they stood and the clothes they wore told a story that could be read by forensic scientists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the kidnapping and rape of an 18-year-old Ohio woman is aided when the victim returns hours after the incident occurred. With her help, the police are able to track the assailant Craig Bailey to his front door.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1981, New York correctional officer Donna Payant disappeared and was later found in a landfill. The medical examiner not only identified the cause of death, but also found an important clue.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Russ Stager, a popular gym teacher, is found dead of what appears to be an accidental gunshot wound, his family becomes suspicious. They think his wife may have planned his murder. When police find that her previous husband died in questionable circumstances, they re-examine the crime scene and uncover evidence that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Exploring how an American hit single from 1966 -- "The Cheater" by Bob Kuban and the In-Men -- foreshadowed the demise of the group's lead singer, Walter Scott.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Krista Harrison is the 11-year-old murder victim of Robert Anthony Buell. There are few clues to the killer's identity, but authorities are intrigued by unusual orange fibers embedded in clothing found near the victim. Months go by and the case goes cold, until a van turns up with what appear to be the same orange fibers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man died in his home after a long history of heart disease. It was assumed that this was the cause of his death. A few weeks after the funeral, rumors surfaced that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Residents of Noel, Missouri were stunned to learn that their bank had been robbed and the bank president was missing. His body was later found floating in a lake, securely bound to a chair with duct tape.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Haunted by the disappearance of her mother, a young woman undertook an investigation of her own. Her mother's diary was in the cold case file; there, she discovered a dark family secret, which might have been the reason her mother vanished.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Seattle policeman Matt Bachmeier's house goes up in flames, fire experts suspect arson, possibly committed by someone with a vendetta against the officer. One month later, the officer whose home was torched, arrests low-level drug dealer James Wren, who confesses to the arson. But when the suspect vanishes, investigators start to question the authenticity of the so-called confession.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1962, the people of the small town of Hanford, California lost their sense of peace when one of their own, 15-year-old Marlene Miller, was murdered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of a wealthy American businessman was found in his rental car. Teeth and bone fragments were the only remains. When the victim's wife files an insurance claim for $7 million, investigators seek the help of a renowned forensic anthropologist.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of a mail-order bride proves suspicious when detectives learn that two of the Texas husband's previous wives had died in mysterious circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two people in Seattle, Washington died after taking an over-the-counter pain reliever; lab analysis of the pills showed they were tainted with a lethal concentration of cyanide. The investigation which followed led police to a suspect with a motive for murder and a callous disregard for others.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of Katie Poirier is stymied by the lack of a body, but they find a few charred remains including a tooth. The tooth's filling contains unique properties that help investigators identify her remains. Further forensic evidence is then used to convict Donald Blom of her murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Colorado hunter who'd been shot three times was the victim of a well planned crime. Investigators suspected two weapons and two killers. But analysis of the forensic evidence pointed them in a different direction--one much closer to home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An examination of the 2002 DC area shooting spree that resulted in ten murders.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspicious fire swept through an apartment killing two young women. The cause of the fire and the identity of the victims were unclear. But a closer look at the fire scene revealed something hidden in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Deadly Knowledge The investigation into a missing college coed leads police to discover that, unbeknownst to her friends, she lived a bizarre double life, attending school by day and working as a call-girl by night. When her body is found, investigators gather evidence that implicates her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A look back at the case of the River Park Rapist, who assaulted four women in South Bend, Ind., during 1996. Law enforcement officers arrested Richard Alexander, who was convicted in 1998 and sentenced to 70 years in prison.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A healthy young man dies mysteriously in his sleep, and toxicology tests reveal a lethal dose of lidocaine in his blood. Investigators find a suicide note, and presume he killed himself until a forensic linguist examines the note.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Sheila Bryan, who was convicted of killing her mother in a car fire, is granted a new trial. An expert fire witness's opinion on how the fire ignited differs from the prosecution's theory. If verified, it would clear Bryan of any culpability.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, nine-year-old Valiree Jackson vanished on her way to school near Spokane, WA. Her father Brad Jackson reported the disappearance and the entire community started searching for her abductor.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An examination of the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case. When the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife Shirley Duguay was discovered, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman identifies her husband as her attacker, but questions arise 16 years later regarding her recollections.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The disappearance of Philadelphia college athlete Aimee Willard is investigated after her car was found on the roadside. When her body is found, the police find unusual marks on her body and DNA evidence that eventually lead to her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, California firefighters had battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the southern California fires to several fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared and her husband became the prime suspect -- especially after police found a huge bloodstain on the carpet of their bedroom. When they sprayed the bedroom with Luminol, they discovered it was awash with blood spatter. Complex DNA testing proved it was the wife's blood. Now all they had to do was find her body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A suspicious fire swept through an apartment killing two young women. The cause of the fire and the identity of the victims were unclear. But a closer look at the fire scene revealed something hidden in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Deadly Knowledge The investigation into a missing college coed leads police to discover that, unbeknownst to her friends, she lived a bizarre double life, attending school by day and working as a call-girl by night. When her body is found, investigators gather evidence that implicates her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young woman suddenly becomes critically ill, eventually leaving her unable to walk. A bone marrow test reveals the cause: arsenic. Investigators must determine if the poisoning was caused by groundwater contamination or something more sinister.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A look back at a trio of unsolved murders that occurred in Wichita Falls, Texas during the 1980s, details how a fourth murder from the same time period provided the police with more than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, a small community in upstate New York was devastated when Patty Wlasiuk died in a truck accident. But burdock seed pods found in her hair and on her clothes would indicate that this was no accident. It was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A look back at the case of the River Park Rapist, who assaulted four women in South Bend, Ind., during 1996. Law enforcement officers arrested Richard Alexander, who was convicted in 1998 and sentenced to 70 years in prison.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her car; the scene had been staged to make murder appear to be suicide. During their investigation, police discovered the victim was involved in a love quadrangle, giving several people a motive to kill her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When 19-year-old Lori Auker left her Pennsylvania home for work and never arrived, it took space-age technology, cat hairs and insects to pinpoint the image of the woman's abductor.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of 16-year-old Leanne Tiernan was discovered nine months after she disappeared. Forensic scientists found clues that painted a virtual portrait of her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman's death in Pennsylvania triggers a homicide investigation into another woman's death in North Carolina. The similarities in the cases were striking, and examiners must determine if the suspect's story about accidental drowning is all wet.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A talented television news anchor was shot to death outside her home; it appeared to be a crime of passion, perpetrated by an obsessed fan.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teen was killed by her parents in what they said was self-defense, but forensic evidence told otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Some of the refugees who sought asylum in the United States after World War II lied about the atrocities in which they'd participated. Years later, when a high-ranking religious figure was suspected of war crimes, there seemed to be no way to prove his guilt or innocence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two people in Seattle, Washington died after taking an over-the-counter pain reliever; lab analysis of the pills showed they were tainted with a lethal concentration of cyanide. The investigation which followed led police to a suspect with a motive for murder and a callous disregard for others.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An examination of the 2002 DC area shooting spree that resulted in ten murders.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police in the Great Plains are called about a dead body, they do a background check on the victim which leads them into a bizarre web of homeless drifters, cattle auctions and bad checks _ all fronted by elderly couple Ray and Faye Copeland.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, Police received a call from Darlie Routier telling them of her two sons' murders. However, police were unsure about the validity of Darlie's story and began an intensive search of the crime scene. The police determined who the killer was by analyzing blood spatter, doing behavioral profiling and analyzing Darlie's 911 call.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man disappears one year to the day after beginning an affair with his friend's wife. 15 years later, with the help of a metal detector, police found evidence which would prove what had happened, and who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When 180 Legionnaires contract pneumonia-like symptoms after a Philadelphia Convention and 29 of them die, doctors and scientists are mystified. The determination of one scientist helps to determine the cause and likely vector of this deadly disease.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In this classic episode of Forensic Files, the longest running true crime series in television history, a serial bomber was waging a vendetta against the legal profession: A judge, two attorneys and even a courthouse had been targeted. Employing a range of forensic techniques, investigators used evidence from the bombs to lead them to the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Colorado hunter who'd been shot three times was the victim of a well planned crime. Investigators suspected two weapons and two killers. But analysis of the forensic evidence pointed them in a different direction--one much closer to home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the apparent overdose suicide of Reverend Bill Guthrie's wife Sharon. Detectives suspect foul play after they recover incriminating files from Bill's computer and learn of his infidelity.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One year after her father's death, a teen's role in a high school production of "Hamlet" causes her to reveal the chilling truth behind her family's tragedy.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On September 17, 1984, in a suburb of Tucson, Arizona, eight-year-old Vicki Hoskins left home on her pink bicycle to mail a letter for her mother. She never returned, but her slightly damaged bicycle was found nearby. Investigators turned to forensic science, in the hope it would tell them not only what happened to Vicki, but also who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, Robert Curley was diagnosed with Guillain_Barr_ syndrome when he arrived at the hospital with excruciating leg pains. However, doctors became suspicious during Robert's second admission and tested him for Thallium poisoning, which proved positive and Robert died shortly thereafter. Autopsy showed that Robert ingested more poison a few days before his death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One investigator's quick thinking helps him capture a shoe impression made in snow before the evidence melts away. The shoe leads investigators to Peter Benedict and Frank Lanoue, who are convicted of aggravated assault and robbery.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two different men called police to report the same murder. Apparently, neither one knew that the other had called. The investigation uncovered even more unusual circumstances.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Following an outbreak of thyrotoxicosis in Sioux Falls, S.D., thought to have come from contaminated meat, the Department of Agriculture conducts a review of the use of thyroid hormones in animal products.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In Pennsylvania 1984, a passer-by finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. Investigators rely on insect activity analysis by a forensic entomologist to determine when the murder victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On October 15, 1985, two bomb explosions rocked Salt Lake City and resulted in two deaths. A third explosion occurred the next day; this time, the victim was injured but survived. As the investigation progressed, police came to believe the survivor was more than an innocent by-stander.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, nine-year-old Valiree Jackson vanished on her way to school near Spokane, WA. Her father Brad Jackson reported the disappearance and the entire community started searching for her abductor.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For more than a decade, women in a small Louisiana city lived in fear of a rapist who became so experienced, he left no clues to his identity. But computer technology and behavioral science combine to give police a new forensic tool: geographical profiling.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-man shrimp boat sank when a fast-moving, violent storm struck the Louisiana coast. The captain drowned, and the deckhand was rescued fourteen hours later. The deckhand was considered a hero until the captain's body was found.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1980, Michigan resident Shannon Mohr died in what was reported to be a horseback riding accident by her new husband David Davis. After her death, Shannon's family reported to police their suspicions that Davis seemed far from grief-stricken.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the kidnapping and rape of an 18-year-old Ohio woman is aided when the victim returns hours after the incident occurred. With her help, the police are able to track the assailant Craig Bailey to his front door.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1989, Nova Scotia schoolteacher Clayton Johnson left for work. Later that morning, Mrs. Molloy arrived to drop off her child at the Johnson home. She found Clayton's wife Janice Johnson lying at the bottom of the basement stairs in a pool of blood and it was determined to be an accident. Later on, Clayton Johnson married a 22-year-old member of his Pentecostal congregation. When homicide investigator Brian Oldford heard this, he became suspicious and decided to reinvestigate.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1993, the Amtrak Railroad experienced the deadliest train crash in United States history when the Sunset Limited derailed while crossing Alabama's Big Bayou Canot bridge. Forty-seven passengers and crew were killed; scores more were injured.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Joann Katrinak is found dead in a corn field with her infant son Alex next to her, the obvious suspect is the husband and father of the victims. But some insects found on the bodies reveal a vital clue, as does a long blonde hair found on the victims. The blonde hair has something uniquely in common with the brunette hair of the husband's former love interest Patricia Rorrer. The link uncovers a twisted story of passion gone wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the series premiere of Forensic Files, the longest running true crime series in television history, a Connecticut flight attendant went missing and was never seen again. Police suspected her husband was guilty of murder and they were able to prove it - even though they never found the woman's body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of 9-year-old Jessica Knott is investigated. Investigators use a garbage bag to connect suspect James Edward Crow, Jr. to the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1998, an evening out at a Maryland murder mystery theatre performance turns into a real life whodunit when the badly burned body of Stephen Hricko is discovered in his hotel room after a fire.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1987 disappearance of Houston resident Tracy Jo Shine is recalled. The investigation went cold until 2000 when a 'cold case squad' learned that the woman's ex-boyfriend Michael Neal had bragged about killing her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In a search for the killer of two teenagers in Texas, a behavioral profile led to suspect Jason Massey _ and hard science proved the profile was correct. Massey was executed by lethal injection on April 3, 2001.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An Alaskan police officer found a woman's body while on routine patrol of a public park. The victim had been stabbed to death and, during the autopsy, the medical examiner preserved the portion of her rib cage which sustained the fatal wound.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1983, two young boys were found tied and murdered with similar wounds within three months of each other in two Nebraska towns. The rope used to tie the boys was unique. Another clue turned up when one of the boy's brother was hypnotized and remembered a uniformed man in a tan car.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, Maine resident Pearl Smith is missing after an argument with her husband Bill Bruns. But when an investigation turns up a blood trail that leads to the couple's basement, police are sure they'll find the victim's body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young TV news producer is raped and murdered in her apartment. Police identify two suspects, but both are cleared of any wrongdoing. The case stalls for more than a year, and then investigators turn to the Commonwealth of Virginia's DNA Databank.

programme.title: Poisonous Liaisons
programme.desc: Feel the fury that drove a spurned lover and an angry wife to get toxic revenge.

programme.title: Poisonous Liaisons
programme.desc: Get a taste of cold, calculated revenge delivered by two troubled women.

programme.desc: Find out how a prank 911 call puts firefighters and paramedics in danger.

programme.desc: Unravel a tangled web of friendship and murder that leads to a single suspect.

programme.title: ATL Homicide
programme.desc: Quinn and Vince are called to investigate the death of man found in a trap house in a suburban neighborhood. While uncovering nicknames and chasing down leads, Vince will find himself in a dangerous standoff to bring in the killer.

programme.title: ATL Homicide
programme.desc: A casual summer morning turns deadly as a man is killed in a drive-by shooting. To solve this case, Quinn and Vince must build trust with the community and key witnesses to discover the leads they need to find the killer's true motive and identity.

programme.title: Payback
programme.desc: When a man is shot and killed soon after an alleged domestic assault, authorities suspect the girlfriend, but through their investigation they learn she did indeed seek payback, but it was not intended to be deadly.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: When 34-year-old Julii Johnson is gunned down in front of her boyfriend’s home, police are baffled by the lack of a motive – until they find half a million dollars hidden in her boyfriend’s condo. Was the murder payback by a rival drug dealer? Or did this “It Girl” finally make the wrong woman jealous?

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: It seemed nothing could tear these three best friends apart. But behind closed doors trouble was brewing, and things would boil over in the most horrifying way. Now it’s up to detectives to determine what triggered the bloodshed? And who’s to blame?

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: When a mother of three disappears in the middle of the night, her boyfriend and children have little to offer police. Will a mysterious friend be able to lead them to her? Or will the search for her end on the banks of a raging river?

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: When the bodies of a missing couple surface in the waters of New Orleans, detectives wonder who could have committed this heinous crime. Their investigation leads them to a young woman who was so desperate, she�d do anything for her man.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: A close-knit community is gripped with fear after two families are found brutally murdered. Police work to unravel the mystery and are led down a terrifying trail of violent lies, proving you can�t trust anyone, even the ones you love the most.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: When a young model meets a charming, handsome man on the heels of losing her father, her future suddenly seems brighter. But soon, she finds herself following him down a dark road, destroying anyone who gets in their way � all in the name of love.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: A heartbroken young mother is elated to get a second chance at love when she meets the man of her dreams � until she discovers her new lover has a dark side. Before long, she finds her life launched into chaos when she sets out to prove she�s the ride-or-die woman that her man demands.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: Friends and family are stunned when a beautiful college student drops everything to run away with her new man. When she suddenly returns home nine months later, she reveals the shocking story of a brutal murder, notorious drug kingpin, and the destructive desire at the center of her secret life.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: A truck driver vanishes just months into her marriage, and detectives wonder if she�s having second thoughts. However, crucial evidence points investigators toward a sinister plot spanning multiple states that ends with a horrific discovery.

programme.title: ATL Homicide
programme.desc: A man is found murdered in his truck in Northwest Atlanta, and Quinn and Vince are on the case. But when one eyewitness contradicts another, the detectives will have to sift through fact and fiction to bring this killer to justice.

programme.title: ATL Homicide
programme.desc: When a detective in New York calls Atlanta Police about a case, Quinn and Vince take the call. In doing so, the detectives reopen a cold case about a woman found dead in her apartment. To catch this killer, they'll need to take a trip to New York.

programme.title: Payback
programme.desc: After a woman miraculously survives 17 gunshot wounds, Bellwood, Illinois police, aided by witness statements and video surveillance, uncover a triangle of romance, rage and revenge.

programme.desc: Dayton, Ohio is on edge after a spree of random shootings terrorize the city. Police frantically search for clues and uncover evidence that the brutal crimes may be connected to a band of unlikely young killers and a teenage love gone wrong.

programme.title: For My Man

programme.title: For My Man

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: A frantic 911 call leads investigators to the scene of a brutal double-homicide, just outside of Atlanta. programme.title: Bloomberg Open Interest
programme.desc: Delivering the market-moving news anyone needs to know to get a jump start on the U.S. trading day.

The program brings the audience interviews from the biggest names in the business, offering in-depth analysis and critical insights.

programme.title: Bloomberg Real Yield
programme.desc: Bloomberg Real Yield, is a weekly fixed-income show driven by Bloomberg data and Wall Street analysis. Each week guests from major bond companies join us to give their insight and perspective on how to trade the global headlines.

programme.title: Bloomberg: Balance of Power
programme.desc: Focusing on the intersection of politics and global business.

programme.title: Bloomberg Businessweek Daily
programme.desc: Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec bring reporting from the magazine that helps global leaders stay ahead, plus insight on the people, companies and trends shaping today's complex economy.

programme.title: Bloomberg: The Close
programme.desc: Alix Steel, Romaine Bostick and Scarlet Fu own the market-moving breaking news around the closing bell; a synthesis of the top stories; key insights from investors, strategists and the companies of today and tomorrow.

programme.title: Bloomberg: Balance of Power
programme.desc: Focusing on the intersection of politics and global business.

programme.desc: Candid conversations with CEOs, investors, and government leaders focused on how to navigate and prosper in this age of economic uncertainty. Hosted by Erik Schatzker in Singapore.

programme.desc: Mishal Husain brings her signature blend of curiosity and tenacity to weekly conversations with world leaders, business titans and cultural icons, revealing who they really are and how they see the world changing around them.

programme.title: Leaders with Francine Lacqua: The Podcast
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua speaks with Emma Grede, chief executive and co-founder of Good American and founding partner of Skims. They discuss leading with radical honesty, hiring flexible talent for different stages of growth and why failure, directness and self-belief are essential to scaling a business. She also opens up about stepping into the spotlight and shares her views on work from home, modern leadership and the role of artificial intelligence in retail.

programme.desc: "El Puma" secuestra al hijo de su hermano Manuel por las tierras de "El Paraíso." Manuel se une a Autodefensas para hacer justicia.

programme.title: La banda del ántrax
programme.desc: Un expolicía está al frente de una peligrosa organización dedicada al secuestro, conocida como La banda del ántrax. Un día, secuestra a dos mujeres, por lo que llegará a enfrentar a un viejo compañero, causante de su retiro como policía y también de su próxima desgracia.

programme.title: Noches violentas
programme.desc: Varias mujeres son encontradas asesinadas y como el asesino logra escapar en todas las ocasiones, se culpa a una persona inocente.

programme.title: Angela
programme.desc: Angela es una reconocida agente judicial porque tiene la destreza de encontrar a los criminales más buscados, pero su peor enemigo es su pasado.

programme.title: Sinvergüenzas
programme.desc: Dos ladrones malhumorados e ineptos roban accidentalmente la motosierra de un mafioso y ahora luchan para mantenerse a salvo de su venganza.

programme.title: Por una mujer casada
programme.desc: Ir por la vida buscando aventuras con cualquier mujer no es raro, pero buscar solamente mujeres casadas trae aún más problemas.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: Maria do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Tras la acusación de Mahinur, Melissa decide huir de la casa. Los niños ayudan a Cuneyt en la venta de manzanas. Metin busca a Melissa.

programme.title: El laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Alicia Vega, psicóloga del colegio Los Ángeles, especialista en prevención y tratamiento de abusos sexuales a menores, inicia una investigación cuando Valentina, una niña adorable y frágil, parece estar en la mira de un abusador.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Jade y Lucas no pueden irse a vivir juntos, porque Leonardo no los apoya, ella subraya que dejó todo por él. Lucas piensa en Albieri, pero él está aterrado, se trata de la sobrina de Alí.

programme.title: V.I.P
programme.desc: Na Jung-Sun vivía una vida envidiable, junto a Park Sung-Joon, un marido perfecto. Un día recibe un mensaje que dice que su esposo está teniendo una aventura.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Jacob le dice al mensajero de su familia que ya no quiere poner a nadie más en riesgo por la venganza de Esaú. Isaac está cansado de que Esaú trate de matar a su hermano y le hace una advertencia. Lea está embarazada por segunda vez de Jacob.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Miguel trata de calmar a Álvaro para que deje pasar la boda de Isaura. Leoncio le da regalos a Isaura tratando de compensar su violencia. Belchior confiesa ser virgen. Malvina interrumpe una situación incómoda entre Isaura y Leoncio.
